passive.name: Rebirth
passive.description: Upon taking fatal damage, Anivia reverts to an egg and is reborn with full health.

wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Innate:}} Periodically, upon taking {{tip|death|fatal damage}}, '''Anivia''' enters {{tip|resurrection}} for 6 seconds and restores all of her {{sti|{{as|health}}}}.
wiki_detail.description2: While under resurrection, '''Anivia''' is {{tip|lockout|unable to act}} and gains {{pp|-40 to 20 for 5|1;5;8;12;15|changedisplay=true}} {{sti|armor|{{as|'''bonus''' armor}}}} and {{sti|mr|{{as|'''bonus''' magic resistance}}}}.
wiki_detail.description3: If '''Anivia''' remains alive by the end of the duration, she is revived with her {{as|'''current''' health}}.
wiki_detail.notes:
* Triggering ''Rebirth'' will break {{tip|tether|tethers}} on '''Anivia'''.
* {{ai|Chronoshift|Zilean}} and {{ii|Guardian Angel}} will take priority over ''Rebirth''.
* If ''Rebirth'' triggers while '''Anivia''' is channeling {{si|Teleport}}, her channel won't be interrupted.
** All other {{tip|channel|channels}} will be interrupted upon triggering ''Rebirth''.
* The following refers for interactions while '''Anivia''' is resurrecting:
{{ct|move=false|cast=false|attack=false|items=false|consume=false|spells=false|interrupts=death}}
<!-- Blurb -->
wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Innate:}} Periodically, upon {{tip|death|dying}}, {{ci|Anivia}} will instead revert into an egg. If the egg can survive for a few seconds, she is gloriously {{tip|resurrection|reborn}}.

Q.name: Flash Frost
Q.description: Anivia brings her wings together and summons a sphere of ice that flies towards her opponents, chilling and damaging anyone in its path. When the sphere explodes it does moderate damage in a radius, stunning anyone in the area.
Q.tooltip:
Anivia fires a massive chunk of ice, dealing {{ totalpassthroughdamage }} magic damage and Chilling enemies for {{ slowduration }} seconds, Slowing them by {{ spell.glacialstorm:slowamount }}%. At the end of its range, the ice detonates, Stunning enemies for {{ stunduration }} seconds and dealing {{ totalexplosiondamage }} magic damage.

Anivia can Recast this ability while the ice flies to detonate it early.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Anivia''' launches a chunk of ice in the target direction that deals {{as|magic damage}} to enemies hit and {{tip|slow|slows}} them by {{pp|changedisplay=true|key=%|20;20 to 40 for 3|0 to 3|type = [[File:Glacial Storm.png|20px|border|link=]] ''Glacial Storm's'' Rank|formula = 20%+(10%*(''Glacial Storm'' Rank-1))}} for 3 seconds.
wiki_detail.description2: ''Flash Frost'' can be recast while the ice is in flight after its cast time, and does so automatically at maximum range.
wiki_detail.description3: {{sbc|Recast:}} '''Anivia''' shatters the ice, dealing {{as|magic damage}} to nearby enemies and {{tip|stun|stunning}} them for a duration, as well as refreshing the {{tip|slow}}.
wiki_detail.leveling3:
{{st|Magic Damage|{{ap|60 to 200}} {{as|(+ 45% AP)}}|Total Magic Damage|{{ap|50+60 to 130+200}} {{as|(+ {{ap|45+25}}% AP)}}}}
{{st|Stun Duration|{{ap|1.1 to 1.5}} seconds}}
wiki_detail.notes:
*The initial cast and the manual recast count as ability activations for the purposes of on-cast effects such as {{tip|Spellblade}} and triggering {{ais|Force Pulse|Kassadin}} passive.
** If the ability is not manually recasted, the secondary effect will trigger without being considered as an ability activation.
* ''Flash Frost'' can only proc {{ri|Conqueror}} once even if it damages the same target twice.
* ''Flash Frost'' will fire from wherever '''Anivia''' was at the start of the cast time.
* {{tip|Spell shield}} can only prevent either the collision hit or recast from affecting the target.
<!-- Blurb -->
wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Anivia}} launches a chunk of ice that deals {{as|magic damage}} and {{tip|slows}} enemies hit.
wiki_detail.blurb2: ''Flash Frost'' will recast at max range, or can recast early.
wiki_detail.blurb3: {{sbc|Recast:}} The ice explodes, dealing {{as|magic damage}} and {{tip|stun|stunning}} nearby enemies.

W.name: Crystallize
W.description: Anivia condenses the moisture in the air into an impassable wall of ice to block all movement. The wall only lasts a short duration before it melts.
W.tooltip: Anivia summons a wall of ice {{ e2 }} units wide. The wall lasts for {{ e1 }} seconds before it melts.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Anivia''' summons a wall of ice at the target location perpendicular to her facing, {{tip|airborne|knocking}} all units away from it, though not through terrain. The wall lingers as impassable terrain for 5 seconds.
wiki_detail.leveling:
{{st|{{tt|Width|Pathfinding}}|{{tip|pathing radius|icononly = true}} {{ap|600 to 1000}} units}}
{{st|Number of ice segments|{{ap|4 to 8}} chunks of ice|Distance between outermost segments|{{ap|400 to 800}} units|Distance between individual segments|{{ap|133.33|125|120|116.67|114.29}} units}}
wiki_detail.target range: {{tt|1000|Maximum cast range for wall center}} / {{tt|100|Minimum cast range}}
wiki_detail.notes:
* ''Crystallize's'' displacement triggers in-combat effects by dealing 0 {{tip|proc damage|proc}} {{tip|true damage}}, such as drawing turret aggro, {{ri|Sudden Impact}} and applying {{ii|Elixir of Sorcery}}.
* Individual ice chunks have both 100 units {{tip|pathing radius}} and {{tip|gameplay radius}}.
* {{tip|champion|Champions}} are knocked 120 units to either side of a wall segment, non-champions 250 units.
** {{pending for test}} Knockback speed?
* Player-generated terrain such as ''Crystallize'' does not block {{tip|sight}}.
<!-- Blurb -->
wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Anivia}} {{tip|airborne|erects}} a wall of ice that lingers for a few seconds, blocking movement.

E.name: Frostbite
E.description: With a flap of her wings, Anivia blasts a freezing gust of wind at her target, dealing damage. If the target was recently hit by Flash Frost or damaged by a fully formed Glacial Storm, the damage they take is doubled.
E.tooltip: Anivia blasts an enemy with a freezing wind, dealing {{ totaldamage }} magic damage. Against Chilled enemies, Anivia deals {{ empowereddamage }} magic damage instead.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Passive:}} Enemies hit by ''{{ai|Flash Frost|Anivia}}'' or a fully formed ''{{ai|Glacial Storm|Anivia}}'' become ''Chilled'' for 3 seconds, refreshing on subsequent hits.
wiki_detail.description2: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Anivia''' blasts a freezing wind at the target enemy that deals {{as|magic damage}}, {{ccs|doubled|magic}} if they were ''Chilled''.
wiki_detail.leveling2: {{st|Magic Damage|{{ap|50 to 150}} {{as|(+ 55% AP)}}|Enhanced Damage|{{ap|50*2 to 150*2}} {{as|(+ {{ap|55*2}}% AP)}}}}
wiki_detail.notes:
* The damage of ''Frostbite'' is calculated once it hits. If the target's mark from being hit by {{ai|Flash Frost|Anivia}} or a fully formed {{ai|Glacial Storm|Anivia}} wares off while the projectile is traveling, the damage is not doubled.
* ''Frostbite'' has a different sound effect when it hits a target for double damage.
{{Unit-targeted cancel conditions}}
<!-- Blurb -->
wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Anivia}} blasts a freezing wind at the target enemy that deals {{as|magic damage}}.
wiki_detail.blurb2: If the target had been recently hit by ''{{ai|Flash Frost|Anivia}}'' or ''{{ai|Glacial Storm|Anivia}}'', the damage is {{ccs|doubled|magic}}.

R.name: Glacial Storm
R.description: Anivia summons a driving rain of ice and hail to damage her enemies and slow their advance.
R.tooltip:
Toggle: Anivia calls forth a driving rain of ice and hail that Slows enemies by {{ slowamount }}% and deals {{ totaldamagepersecond }} magic damage per second. The storm increases in size over {{ growthtime }} seconds.

When the storm is fully formed, it Chills, Slows by {{ slowpercentempoweredtt }}%, and does {{ empowereddamagepersecondtooltiponly }} magic damage per second instead.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Anivia''' calls forth a driving rain of ice and hail at the target location, dealing {{as|magic damage}} every {{fd|0.5}} seconds to enemies within and {{tip|slow|slowing}} them for 1 second, refreshing every {{fd|0.5}} seconds while they remain inside.
wiki_detail.leveling:
{{st|Magic Damage per Tick|{{ap|15 to 30}} {{as|(+ {{fd|6.25}}% AP)}}}}
{{st|Slow|{{ap|20 to 40}}%}}
wiki_detail.description2: The blizzard increases in size over {{fd|1.5}} seconds. At maximum size, ''Glacial Storm'' is empowered to deal 300% damage and increase the effectiveness of its slow by 50%, which also instead lasts {{fd|1.5}} seconds and refreshes every {{fd|0.25}} seconds.
wiki_detail.leveling2:
{{st|Empowered Damage per Tick|{{ap|15*3 to 30*3}} {{as|(+ {{ap|6.25*3}}% AP)}}}}
{{st|Empowered Slow|{{ap|20*1.5 to 40*1.5}}%}}
wiki_detail.description3: ''Glacial Storm'' can be recast after 1 second, and does so automatically if '''Anivia''' is no longer in range or unable to pay the {{sti|{{as|mana}}}} cost, or becomes affected by any form of {{tip|interrupt|interrupting}} {{tip|crowd control}}.
wiki_detail.description4: {{sbc|Recast:}} '''Anivia''' ends ''Glacial Storm'', dealing one last tick of damage.
wiki_detail.notes:
*Toggle abilities do not count as ability activations for the purposes of on-cast effects such as {{tip|Spellblade}} and triggering {{ais|Force Pulse|Kassadin}} passive.
* ''Glacial Storm's'' slow leaves a trail that is visible even if the target is [[stealth]]ed.
* ''Glacial Storm'' deals 3 half ticks at {{ap|200|267|333}} radius for a total of {{fd|1.5}} normal damage ticks before it starts dealing empowered damage at 400 radius.
* {{tip|Stasis (buff)|Stasis}} via {{ii|Zhonya's Hourglass}} doesn't interrupt ''Glacial Storm''.
** {{cai|Devour|Tahm Kench}} '''does''' interrupt (allied and enemy) ''Glacial Storm''.
<!-- Blurb -->
wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Toggle:}} {{ci|Anivia}} summons a driving rain of ice and hail at the target location that continually deals {{tip|magic damage}} and {{tip|slow|slows}} enemies within.
wiki_detail.blurb2: ''Glacial Storm'' is deactivated when '''Anivia''' leaves it or gets hit by {{tip|crowd control|disables}}.
